title: Market Brief — Monday, April 6, 2026
summary: Compute is tight across the board. H100 availability sits at a severe 19% (50/270), H200 at 28% (108/391), and B200 at 26% (84/320), none signaling oversupply. H100 spot jumped 9.2% day-over-day to $2.04/hr, the sharpest move in the dataset, signaling rising demand against already scarce supply.
paragraphs: Compute is tight across the board. H100 availability sits at a severe 19% (50/270), H200 at 28% (108/391), and B200 at 26% (84/320), none signaling oversupply. H100 spot jumped 9.2% day-over-day to $2.04/hr, the sharpest move in the dataset, signaling rising demand against already scarce supply.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), self-hosting Llama 70B on H100 runs $7.45/M tokens, making medium-tier API providers sell at steep losses. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is burning cash spectacularly, losing roughly $7.07/M tokens on H100-class hardware. OpenAI grades Neutral, milking premium o3 Pro margins while subsidizing Mini tiers. Anthropic grades Neutral with Haiku underpriced. Google grades Weak. DeepSeek grades Burning Cash.

B200 inference at TCO-adjusted $3.49/M tokens is the only path to profitable mid-tier pricing, making B200 capacity undervalued at current spot rates. o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued relative to GPT-5.4 at $15/M, short premium reasoning tokens and rotate into B200 spot commitments.
bullets: 19% H100 availability means only 50 of 270 GPUs are free, well into severe shortage territory.
108 of 391 H200 GPUs are available at 28%, tight but the loosest pool in the stack.
$2.04/hr H100 spot is up 9.2% in one day, the largest single-day move tracked.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of roughly $7.07/M against TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launched at base 100.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M.
